rubric_items.reasoning: This is a genuine root-cause implementation: real CA/node/client certs generated, stored in the crdb-cluster-certs Secret, and the StatefulSet spec itself changed from --insecure to --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs with the secret mounted and probes switched to HTTPS — so the config is durable across restarts/rescheduling, not a one-off runtime hack. Both goal checks were independently re-verified after the fix (insecure rejected, secure SELECT 1 succeeds) and `cockroach node status --certs-dir=...` confirms all 3 nodes are live on the new certs, directly tying the Secret to what nodes actually serve. Minor deduction because the openssl-toolbox pod called for in the task didn't exist and the agent substituted local openssl generation without flagging this as a possible environment/precondition issue.

The agent applied the StatefulSet change, hit a genuine Kubernetes quirk (rolling updates go one-pod-at-a-time by highest ordinal even under podManagementPolicy: Parallel), correctly diagnosed the resulting mixed-TLS quorum loss from pod state output, and recovered by deleting all 3 pods to force simultaneous recreation against the already-updated spec. This is a real, non-obvious obstacle rather than agent error, and the diagnosis-then-fix was coherent and evidence-driven rather than trial-and-error — but it is still a detour/backtrack from the straight-line path, so it doesn't merit a perfect score.

Commands were scoped to the cockroachdb namespace throughout (99 calls, only 5 mutations, no cross-namespace or destructive actions outside the task). Using openssl directly instead of the (nonexistent) openssl-toolbox pod was a reasonable substitution. Deleting all 3 StatefulSet pods at once is a heavier action than a single targeted patch, but it was a deliberate, minimal-blast-radius fix for a self-inflicted quorum problem within the same namespace/statefulset, not a reckless or out-of-scope mutation.

The change set is focused: one Secret, one StatefulSet edit (args, volume mount, probes), and the pod recreation needed to apply it — only 5 mutations total with no unrelated resources touched. Slight deduction for the extra churn caused by the rolling-update recovery (deleting/recreating all pods) rather than a cleaner single-shot rollout, though this was a necessary consequence of the StatefulSet's own update ordering rather than agent-driven over-engineering.
